betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Modular Storage: Powercell (Low)§f

§aStore and network Redstone Flux across your base with flexible, wrench-configurable cells!§f

The §9Powercell (Low)§f is a modular energy storage block from §6RFTools Power§f. Instead of acting as conventional static batteries, adjacent Powercells link into a single, shared multiblock network, pooling their storage and scaling capacity dynamically without separate storage systems.

§6Powercell Specifications§f:
§b• Storage Capacity§f: Holds up to §e500,000 RF§f of internal energy buffer.
§b• Transfer Capacity§f: Limits energy transfer to §c250 RF/tick§f per side under default conditions.
§b• Network Scaling§f: Link adjacent cells into a single network of up to §c729 blocks§f (a 9×9×9 area). Every additional block in the network multiplies the side transfer rate by a factor of §a+0.25× (25%)§f!
§b• NBT Preservation§f: When broken, the block §aretains all stored energy§f, allowing you to relocate charged cells without power loss.

§6Configuration & Networking§f:
§b• Side I/O Mode§f: §9Right-click§f a face with a §9wrench§f or §9Smart Wrench§f to cycle that side between §bInput§f (blue/accepts power), §cOutput§f (orange/supplies power), and §7Disabled§f (no transfer).
§b• Network Info§f: Look at the block with §bThe One Probe§f or §bWaila§f (or sneak-hover) to display current input/output rates and local energy levels.
§b• Wireless Linking§f: Place a §bPowercell Card§f inside the card slot to wirelessly link it to other cells across distances or dimensions, creating a unified, global energy grid!

§6Crafting§f:
§eThe cell is crafted in a shaped recipe using §b4× Power Core§f (cardinal positions), §b4× Redstone§f (corners), and an §bRFTools Machine Frame§f (center).

§eTip§f: A basic 2×2×2 Powercell (Low) network already transfers §e500 RF/tick§f per side — enough to drive mid-game machinery without needing to upgrade to Medium.
